item.title: US Mortgage Rates Are Little Changed Despite Surging Inflation
item.description: Mortgage rates in the US were little changed even as the escalating energy crisis drives inflation higher.

item.title: Dollar’s Link to Oil Most Positive Ever as Iran Crisis Drags On
item.description: Eleven weeks into a Middle Eastern conflict that has shuttered a critical shipping channel and sent energy prices soaring, the US dollar’s linkage to oil prices is more positive than ever before.

item.title: Cerebras Shares Indicated to Surge 89% After Year’s Top IPO
item.description: Cerebras Systems Inc. shares are indicated to open 89% above their listing price, after the artificial intelligence chipmaker raised $5.55 billion in an upsized and hotly anticipated IPO.

item.title: Brookfield Says It Amassed $2 Billion Bet on SpaceX Ahead of IPO
item.description: Investment firm Brookfield said it owns $2 billion of pre-IPO shares of Elon Musk’s SpaceX, with half on its parent company’s balance sheet and the rest held by other affiliated entities.

item.title: First Brands Windup Should Be Run by Trustee to Lower Costs, US Says
item.description: Bankrupt auto-parts maker First Brands Group should be taken over by a court-supervised trustee who can finish liquidating its assets at a much lower cost than what the company’s high-priced advisers are charging, federal officials argued in a court filing.

item.title: Yen Briefly Rallies as Traders Eye Japan Intervention Risk
item.description: The yen jumped against the dollar on Thursday before falling back as currency traders brace for possible action by Japanese authorities to support the currency.

item.title: Virtu Starts Trading on Kalshi as Event Bets Draw in Wall Street
item.description: Virtu Financial is joining a small but growing list of institutional market-making firms trading on prediction markets, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: IMF to Vote Next Week on Argentina’s Review To Unlock $1 Billion
item.description: The International Monetary Fund’s executive board will meet next week to discuss Argentina’s latest program review that would unlock a $1 billion disbursement, spokesperson Julie Kozack said Thursday.

item.title: Vitol Offers Iraqi Oil Outside Hormuz in Sign Ships Are Exiting
item.description: Vitol Group is offering Iraqi Basrah crude to customers, people with knowledge of the matter said, in a sign that some shipments may have successfully made it out of the Persian Gulf.

item.title: US Farmers Dial Crop Adjusters as Wild Weather Hammers Wheat
item.description: From drought to ping-pong size hail and unseasonable warmth, weather extremes have wheat farmers reaching for their phones to ring their insurance adjusters to assess the viability of their crops.

item.title: Oil Flows Through Hormuz Creep Higher as More Supertankers Exit
item.description: The number of supertankers hauling unsanctioned oil through the Strait of Hormuz has shown signs of rising in recent days, offering limited relief to an oil market that’s suffered the largest supply disruption in history.

item.title: India Condemns Attack and Sinking of Ship in the Gulf of Oman
item.description: India condemned an attack on one of its vessels in the Gulf of Oman, which sunk after it was set ablaze, calling the incident “unacceptable.”

item.title: India Takes More Measures to Curb Gold Imports
item.description: India has further tightened rules for importing gold into the country, as Prime Minister Narendra Modi steps up efforts to defend the rupee amid the Middle East war.

item.title: Private Credit BDC Redemptions Exceed Fundraising for First Time
item.description: Non-traded private credit funds handed more money back to investors than they raised in the first quarter — the first time outflows from the vehicles have surpassed inflows.

item.title: US Import and Export Prices Jump Most Since 2022 on Fuel Costs
item.description: US import and export prices surged in April by the most in four years on oil-market pressures tied to the Iran conflict, adding to evidence of higher inflation in the world’s largest economy.

item.title: Carney Rolls Out Pitch to Double Canada’s Electricity Output
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney published a strategy that aims to double Canada’s electricity generation by 2050, including adjusting its clean electricity rules to give more flexibility on power generation using natural gas.

item.title: Cuba Runs Out of Fuel, Plunging Country Into Darkness and Protests
item.description: Cuba said the country has completely run out of the diesel and fuel oil it needs to keep its power plants running, with civil unrest starting to break out amid a de facto US energy blockade of the communist-run nation.

item.title: Buybacks, Revamps, Deals: Firms Dig Deep to Fix Problem Private Credit Funds
item.description: The troubled private credit funds, by most any measure, are a sliver of the assets of investing giants KKR & Co., BlackRock Inc. and Apollo Global Management Inc.

item.title: TCW Adds Debt of EM Oil Exporters on War’s Lasting Energy Impact
item.description: The oil shock from the Iran war is set to give a lasting boost to government bonds from energy-producing developing nations, according to Christopher Hays, whose fund at TCW Group Inc. is beating its benchmark and most peers this year.

item.title: Russian Oil Price at Highest Since 2023 Boosts Kremlin Windfall
item.description: Russia is getting a further boost from the Iran war as the price for its flagship Urals oil export blend reached the highest since October 2023 for tax purposes this month.

item.title: JPMorgan Strategist Says Winners in Global Stocks Go Beyond AI
item.description: Global stock markets are offering returns that in some cases are beating the S&P 500 Index, with technology companies comprising a small part of some strategies, according to Paul Quinsee at JPMorgan Asset Management.

item.title: Fermi Reworks Board Rules to Thwart Ex-CEO’s Push to Remake Firm
item.description: Texas data-center developer Fermi Inc. changed its rules around director appointments to block efforts by its former chief executive officer to reshape the company’s board.

item.title: Mobile Carriers Join Forces to Boost Coverage in Dead Zones
item.description: AT&T Inc., T-Mobile US Inc. and Verizon Communications Inc. announced a rare joint venture Thursday that aims to make satellite capabilities more widely available to mobile phone customers.

item.title: Africa E-Commerce Giant Jumia to Cut Workforce Due to AI
item.description: Jumia Technologies AG is planning to cut an initial 10% of its workforce of about 2,000 people as the African e-commerce giant implements artificial intelligence across its departments, Chief Executive Officer Francis Dufay said.

item.title: Motorola’s Razr Fold Is a Solid Phone Arriving at the Worst Time
item.description: The company’s first book-style foldable has a lot to recommend it, but a wave of fresh competition is coming soon.

item.title: Newsom’s California Budget Bolstered by Extra Cash from AI Boom
item.description: California Governor Gavin Newsom unveiled a revised budget that shows no deficit for this year and next, as the state draws another boost from the technology and artificial-intelligence boom.

item.title: Soaring Household Defaults Rock Argentina’s Banks and Fintechs
item.description: A sharp rise in household delinquencies is shaking Argentina’s banks and fintech firms, presenting another challenge to President Javier Milei’s ambitious economic overhaul.

item.title: US Retail Sales Moderate as Gas Surge Crimps Budgets
item.description: US retail sales rose at a slower pace in April, suggesting high gasoline prices prompted consumers to temper their spending at some merchants.

item.title: US Stock Futures Gain as AI Trade Drives Tech Rally, Cisco Soars
item.description: US stock futures rose Thursday morning as the artificial intelligence trade continued to drive gains across technology shares, powered by upbeat earnings from Cisco Systems Inc. and an AI IPO.

item.title: Tate & Lyle Gets Up to £2.7 Billion Offer From Ingredion
item.description: Tate & Lyle Plc has received a takeover offer from Ingredion Inc., raising the prospect of another famous brand leaving London’s stock market.
